_comment: Copy this to .claude/settings.local.json (gitignored), fill in your Hyperspell API key, restart Claude Code. Version is pinned; bump deliberately. The bin name is mcp-server inside the scoped package — invoke via -p so npx resolves it. The MCP exposes only two tools (search_docs and execute); execute runs arbitrary TypeScript against the Hyperspell SDK, so we restrict via --code-blocked-methods with regex against SDK method FQNs to enforce Invariant 2 (only convex/reinforceNode.ts may write triage_history memories). Substring match on user-submitted source — guardrail not hard sandbox; defense-in-depth alongside the application-layer enforcement. The MCP server only reads HYPERSPELL_API_KEY; HYPERSPELL_TOKEN and HYPERSPELL_USER_ID from Hyperspell's docs example are inert at the MCP layer.
